name: Alchemize
notes: Run-warping levels of good. Potions are broken with 8 slots now. Stockpile for problem fights. Fish for exact potions you need. Exhaust can be removed. Early shop buy = carries entire run.

name: Anointed
notes: Awkward. Only works in specific draw orders. Seeking rares is niche. Not buying early game. Can draw 3-5 cards so not 'almost never' but very situational.

name: Automation
notes: ~1 energy every 2 turns. Slow to get online. Higher ceiling with draw spam (Silent). Upgrades to 0 cost for power synergies. Bottom of 'good' tier.

name: Beat Down
notes: Really bad. Turn 1 brick (no attacks in discard). Can't pick targets. Random attacks worst type to play. 3 energy too much. Almost never pick.

name: Bolas
notes: Free damage chipping. Once played, never draw again (returns to hand). Good with Kunai/ninja relics. Can enchant for higher ceiling. Rare = expensive to buy. Great if free, meh if paying.

name: Calamity
notes: Awkward boss cheese. 3 cost = no other plays that turn. Worse than Serpent Form. Random attacks = worst card gen type. Can't get rid of generated cards (unlike Necro ethereal gen).

name: Catastrophe
notes: Random = bad. Often plays Strike+Defend or 2 Defends with no selection. If cost 1 would be great. Usually bad from Neow.

name: Dark Shackles
notes: Insane. Multi-hit enemies = blocks 30+ easily. Zero cost block 9 minimum. Artifact rarely matters. Loops with retain. Better than Untouchable (no conditions).

name: Discovery
notes: Pay 1 energy, pick from 3, cheat out 2-3 cost cards. Very flexible - block in hallways, scaling in bosses. One-card solve potential. Top of 'most time good'. Skip if deck already has scaling.

name: Dramatic Entrance
notes: Buffed from 8 to 11 damage. Innate AoE exhaust. Carries entire run from Neow. Like starting with Festive Popper (-1 draw). Can enchant/upgrade. Huge Act 1 bump for being uncommon.

name: Entropy
notes: Worse than Discovery - does nothing turn you play it. Transforms (not exhausts) so you're fishing. Statuses transform to statuses (worse). Can cheese bosses but rare + slow. Situational.

name: Equilibrium
notes: Insane. Best Neow pick. Massive block + Retain your hand = broken. Not rare so cheaper and from Neow. First shop insta-buy. Equi+Convergence = never discard, hold entire deck.

name: Eternal Armor
notes: Overcosted still even after buff (9 plating). Not impactful enough in hallways. Only good if generated at 0 cost. Can't imagine buying.

name: Fasten
notes: Super Footwork. Upgrades to +7 block per Defend. Defense says 12 on them. Don't upgrade (5 is plenty). Completely solves block. +5 dex basically. Numbers don't make sense. Disgusting.

name: Finesse
notes: Buffed from Spire 1. Free 4 block + draw. Not punished by Gremlin Knob. Works with Dex (now colorless). Enables infinites. Never unhappy to take.

name: Fisticuffs
notes: Iron Wave+ basically. Works with Vuln/Str. DOES work when hitting shield (unlike Spire 1). Always get the 7 block. Great Act 1. Falls off endgame (not engine card).

name: Flash of Steel
notes: Buffed from Spire 1 (bigger number). Free damage + draw. Works with Vuln/Str/Kunai/Shuriken/Kusarigama. Zero cost attack that replaces itself = tons of synergies.

name: Gold Axe
notes: Boss solve only. ~3 cards/turn = weak until turn 5-6. Scales too slow for early game. Rare hurts. Top of situational. Maybe average but not always picked.

name: Hand of Greed
notes: 20 damage is big early game. Shop prices cheaper now so gold goes further. Worse than Spire 1. Only want if free. Delayed ROI not worth 200 gold. Average tier.

name: Hidden Gem
notes: Broken broken broken. Top of S tier. One-card boss solve. Random card gains Replay 2 (4 plays with upgrade). Hits powers (got 4 Footworks once). Draw pile = immediate value. Can hit Alchemize for stalling.

name: Impatience
notes: Awkward since Spire 1. Best for infinite setups but every character has better options now. Brick from Neow. 3% brick rate on colorless Neow.

name: Jack of All Trades
notes: Random colorless = better now since colorless is better. Can high roll into good stuff. Not picking from selection like Discovery hurts. Top of situational. Try upgraded version.

name: Jackpot
notes: Ultra desperation pick. Doesn't exhaust so can cheese long fights. Character-specific 0-cost cards. Defect has most synergy (zero-cost archetype). Regent can print unplayable star cards. Niche.

name: Master of Strategy
notes: Really good acceleration. Never skip if free. Bottom of S tier - expensive and only helps deck do what it does. Not output solve in Act 1. Strong but opportunity cost is real.

name: Mayhem
notes: ~1 extra uncontrolled energy. Can randomly Fiend Fire your hand. Boss solve if desperate. Mostly situational. Better from card gen than buying.

name: Mind Blast
notes: Buffed to 1 cost (was 2). ~10 damage early. Scales but wrong direction (want engine not frontload in Act 3). Hard to justify buying/starting. Worse than Spire 1.

name: Nostalgia
notes: Weird. First attack/skill goes to draw pile top. -1 draw effectively. If looping one card, probably already built small deck. Rare hurts. Almost never. Would be situational if uncommon.

name: Omnislice
notes: Scales with Vuln. Regent/Clad = 12 damage read. Can loop. Zero cost AoE = strong early. Almost solves Act 1 from Neow. Not useful past first half of run. Works with Kunai/Shuriken.

name: Panache
notes: Unchanged from Spire 1. Need to proc 5 cards/turn reliably - Silent Shivs mainly. Clad can't proc (no 0-cost, has 2-cost). Less needed now - everyone has AoE options. Average tier.

name: Panic Button
notes: S tier. 30 block for 0. 'Can't block next turn' = play it, block huge hit, kill next turn. Same damage as blocking 10 twice but make progress. Works with powers that block (Child of Stars). Fixed enemy patterns = know when playable.

name: Prep Time
notes: 4 Vigor/turn stacks. Necrobinder + Lethality = big. Multi-hit attacks synergy. Flesh mentioned. Average - plays better than expected. Later game you have better answers.

name: Production
notes: Nerfed (was upgrade to not exhaust, now +3 energy). Still good burst energy for powers. Like upgraded Seeing Red. Can float energy so not always full value. Can add 'not exhaust' enchant for infinite. Silent + Bag of Prep = sick. Regent least wants (star costs).

name: Prolong
notes: Slept on. Carry block to next turn. 2 Defends + Prolong = block 10 next turn free. Endgame = 30 block carry. Prolong + Panic Button = sick. Scales throughout run. Upgrades to not exhaust.

name: Prowess
notes: Stupid card. +1 Str +1 Dex (upgrades to +2/+2). Footwork + Inflame in one card. Every character has both now. Boss solve. Hallway good. Solves scaling with one card.

name: Purity
notes: Has Retain now (buff). Exhaust synergies (Clad). Thin deck for looping. Need draw to make work. Bad early - minus one draw. Frog parasite status removal. End game purchase mostly. Average.

name: Rend
notes: Number not high enough. Clad has Cinder (24 for 2, no downside). Stacking debuffs = already winning. Artifact fights = sad. Almost never buying.

name: Restlessness
notes: Almost never plays. Never had this work ever. Empty hand is very hard. Reads poorly. Almost never.

name: Rolling Boulder
notes: Slow boss kill. Delayed AOE like Doom. Upgrade only increases flat damage (not scaling). Can kill boss and that's it. Situational.

name: Salvo
notes: Bottom of good tier. Retain keyword is broken. 12 damage is fine. Rare hurts (costs more, can't see from Neow). Would be top if uncommon. Pay for Retain.

name: Scrawl
notes: Draw until hand full (4-5 usually). Rare and doesn't upgrade to 0 cost (like Watcher version). Not strong early. Later game pickup. With Borrowed Time/Production = good. Lined up Prepared+Sly = makes sense.

name: Secret Technique
notes: Very strong. People sleep on tutoring. Seek skills (usually combo pieces). Can get Production for energy. Buy fairly frequently. Skills are main things to tutor after powers.

name: Secret Weapon
notes: Much worse than Secret Technique. Attacks not usually what you want to tutor. Rare hurts. Regent can tutor star payoff. Pommel Strike on Clad = better. Average tier.

name: Seeker Strike
notes: Awkward. Random selection from 3 cards in draw pile. 9 damage for 1 = not great past early game. Rather not add card that's one draw from powers. Average.

name: Shockwave
notes: Ironclad card from Spire 1 unchanged. 3 Weak + 3 Vuln AoE. Why is this off Clad? Perma-weak perma-vuln every character. From Neow = insane. Shop = insane. Late game = insane.

name: Splash
notes: Get attacks from other characters, free to play this turn. Can solve boss with scaling attack. Cheat energy (Hyperbeam, Meteor Strike). Top of 'most time good'. Don't pick Osty attacks (can't use without Osty). Similar to Discovery.

name: Stratagem
notes: Weird. On shuffle, choose card from draw pile. Doesn't work until turn 3-4. Voltaic synergy? Consistency power that works AFTER first cycle. Strange.

name: The Bomb
notes: Meme'd in Spire 1 but buffed (rare→uncommon). 40 delayed AOE = like Doom. Sit and block, make progress. If no output solve, big number. 1 energy = 20 damage rate. Ok from Neow. Top of average.

name: The Gambit
notes: 50 block but die if take unblocked attack. More uses than people think. Barricade enabler. People sleeping on it. Situational but can play.

name: Thinking Ahead
notes: Not draw positive - card selection. Like Retain in weird way. Top deck manipulation (Regent). Feel No Pain synergy. Bottom of 'most time good'. Helps line up combos but not drawing closer to stuff.

name: Thrumming Hatchet
notes: 11 damage (14 upgraded), returns to hand. Never redraw = consistency fix. Uncommon = can see early. First shop buy performs well. Strong early, not wanted Act 2-3. Bottom of 'most time good'.

name: Ultimate Defend
notes: 11 block = insanely high rate. Works with Fasten. Big dense card. Saves million HP. From Neow = really happy. Most time good.

name: Ultimate Strike
notes: 14 damage = big but worse than Thrumming Hatchet. No other effect, no consistency fix, don't want to redraw. Average from Neow but not exciting.

name: Volley
notes: X cost = weird. Random targeting = bad. Not AoE, smaller number. Works with Str/Chem X. Can't target = much worse. Early damage but situational.
